content:
What protocol have you used to connect to the server in each application (imap or pop3)?

  • K9 Mail
  • Thunderbird Android
  • Thunderbird desktop on Mac

imap keeps client(s) and server synchnonised. Deletions performed on a client app are deleted on the server. The deletion will also be applied on any other clients connecting using imap.

Pop3 doesn't support synchronisation. It downloads new emails and stores them locally on the client / device. When you delete an email, only the local app's copy is removed.

It sounds like you have K9 setup to use POP3 and Thunderbird for Android using Imap.

You can check which protocol the mobile apps are using by navigating to ... Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server

Server Name = Can very for imap and pop3 Port 993 = imap port 995 = pop3
creator: platform34

content:
Q1) Where does Thunderbird for Android save POP3 Mailboxes?

The mailbox data is stored within protected internal storage (app sandbox). It's not accessible unless you have a rooted phone.

Q2) Is there a Backup-Restore Feature for POP3 Mailboxes?

Thunderbird for Android doesn't currently offer any backup or restore mechanism for mailbox data.

You can add your idea(s) on Mozilla connect and receive votes from the community. The TB team take these into consideration when planning feature additions and roadmaps. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android
creator: platform34

content:
Are you using an older version of Android?

A beta has recently been released that fixes crashes when retrieving images from large emails. It will make it's way into the next stable release soon. I suspect It may resolve the issue for you. https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/releases/tag/THUNDERBIRD_18_0b3

If the issue is still present after the next update, or you think the issue is different, please complete a bug report on github with a debug log attached.

Submit bug report here: https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues

Guide to collect debug log: https://thunderbird.github.io/thunderbird-android/docs/latest/user-guide/troubleshooting/collecting-debug-logs.html

content:
I'd recommend breaking the issue down and focusing on a single client/server interaction at a time. As this is the TB Android forum, lets start with the mobile app.

Q1) Have you changed either of the below app settings related to deletion handling?

Settings > account > fetching mail > a) When I delete a message:: [Delete from server] b) Erase deleted messages on server:: [Immediately]

Q2) Not quite sure what you mean by "after Check Mail command which syncs to Earthlink servers" Have you visually checked on the server's webmail to confirm if deletion occured, or only checked sync logs and other clients?

Q3) Do you have a trash folder setup or are you expecting the email to be permanently deleted?

Q4) Have you checked what settings are setup on your providers webmail for Imap deletion handling? See the attached from gmail web settings as example.
creator: platform34

content: I have been testing with multiple clients, 2 Android, 1 PC, 1 web client (Firefox) to https://webmail1.earthlink.net. All clients and Firefox are current as are the Pixel 9, Samsung S8 & Win11 Pro OS. Connections are imap. The account is very large, >3500 in Inbox, many folders. If I delete emails on the web client, they show as deleted on Android after TB sync. If I delete on either the Android or PC Thunderbird clients (or multiple TB clients) and TB sync, it remains as current on webmail (after Check Mail command which syncs to Earthlink servers) and other TB clients. i.e. local only. The same with moving emails to folders. Web side works, clients do local only. Also checked settings for TB sync - manual. I don't know if it's a setting or E-Link handles clients differently.
creator: markmue

content:
As a first step, you should exclude the app from any Android battery optimisations and enable the app to run in the background.

The setting are typically available by long pressing the TB launcher icon > app info > battery. The options and menu location can vary by Android version and phone manufacturer.
